Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The purple blocks didn't change. The green blocks are still there. Let me check for green blocks.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        cr = sum(p[0] for p in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
        cc = sum(p[1] for p in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
        print(n['id'], n['color'], cr, cc, n['pixels'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
